data.content:
Planning summary

Current state
- `src/app/beats/use-agent-info-map.ts` seeds Active rows from `beat.metadata.knotsHandoffCapsules` and only then overrides from running terminals. That is the current incorrect source of truth for Agent/Model/Version.
- `src/lib/backends/knots-backend-mappers.ts` maps notes, capsules, and steps into `Beat.metadata`, but it does not currently surface Knots `lease_agent` data from `kno show --json`.
- `src/app/beats/to-active-agent-info.ts` already contains the display normalization needed to split agent/model/version once the input comes from canonical lease fields.

Implementation plan
1. Extend Knots beat mapping so active beats expose lease metadata on the beat model/metadata using lease `agent_name`, `model`/`lease_model`, and `model_version`/`version`, without relying on handoff capsules, step metadata, or terminal labels. Primary files: `src/lib/backends/knots-backend-mappers.ts`, `src/lib/knots.ts`, and mapper coverage.
2. Refactor `src/app/beats/use-agent-info-map.ts` so the Active screen builds `agentInfoByBeatId` from beat lease metadata first and uses terminal state only as a live reflection of the same lease identity while a session is running. Remove capsule authority for the Agent/Model/Version columns and fall back to empty values when no lease metadata exists.
3. Keep `src/app/beats/to-active-agent-info.ts` as the single formatter for column display, but update callers/tests so its inputs are lease `agent_name`, `lease_model`/`model`, and `version` rather than synthesized labels.
4. Add automated coverage by extending `src/lib/__tests__/knots-backend-coverage-tobeat.test.ts` for mapped lease metadata and adding/updating beats-page tests (current `src/app/beats/__tests__/page-active-agent-info.test.ts`, or a new `use-agent-info-map` test) for representative Claude, Codex, and OpenCode leases so Agent/Model/Version stay separated and never collapse into strings like `Claude Opus 4.6`.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium. The scope is localized, but it spans backend mapping plus Active-view projection.
- Dependency: `foolery-4973` is blocked by `foolery-0768`, which is standardizing canonical lease identity creation. This change should consume those canonical lease fields and not re-derive identity from UI labels.
- Main risk: stale fallback behavior. If no active lease exists, the Active screen should render blanks/em dashes rather than stale capsule-derived identity.

Test strategy
- Targeted Vitest runs for backend mapper coverage and active-agent display coverage during implementation.
- Full implementation-step quality gates before any commit: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, and `bun run build`.

Child-knot plan
- No additional child knots proposed. The work is small enough to remain in one implementation beat once the `foolery-0768` lease contract is available.
